fixture: Parent organizing daily life and family memory
self_description: I am a parent and most evenings I am trying to make dinner from what is already in the fridge. We change recipes constantly, then forget the version everyone actually liked. I also have family stories and old recipe notes scattered everywhere. I want the household to feel easier without turning it into a rigid system.

possibilities.label: Fridge rescue
possibilities.why_fit: Dinner begins with what is already in your fridge, not with an ideal shopping list.
possibilities.what_they_do: Make a flexible meal helper that asks what needs using and remembers substitutions your household liked.
possibilities.first_10_30_minutes: A local form and three useful meal paths from tonight's ingredients.

possibilities.label: Family recipe trail
possibilities.why_fit: Your recipes carry family stories, but both the story and the successful variation get scattered.
possibilities.what_they_do: Turn one recipe note into a living page with its origin, variations, reactions, and unanswered memories.
possibilities.first_10_30_minutes: One polished family recipe page and a reusable capture template.

possibilities.label: Gentle household rhythm
possibilities.why_fit: You want daily life to be easier without installing a rigid productivity system.
possibilities.what_they_do: Identify one recurring household bottleneck and design a lightweight cue that preserves improvisation.
possibilities.first_10_30_minutes: A one-week experiment with one cue, one fallback, and no chore dashboard.

possibilities.label: Surprise me
possibilities.why_fit: This combines meal improvisation with family memory rather than treating them as separate chores.
possibilities.what_they_do: Create a dinner-story generator that pairs a flexible recipe prompt with one family question worth preserving.
possibilities.first_10_30_minutes: Tonight's ingredient-based prompt, one story question, and a simple page to save both answers.
